# Difference in differences

::: {.callout-note}
## Difference in differences in a figure

![In this example, difference-in-differences compares the change in outcomes for the **treated group** before and after the policy with the change in outcomes for the **control group** over the same period. The control group’s trend is used to construct the treated group’s **counterfactual** outcome: what would have happened to the treated group in the absence of the policy. The **DiD estimate** is the gap between the treated group’s observed post-policy outcome and this counterfactual outcome.](../images/DiD_in_a_figure.png)
:::

## The evaluation problem

## Key intuition

## Identification assumptions

## Estimation

## Diagnostics and validity checks

## Common mistakes

## Worked example

## How to report the results
